_metadata.title: Security Scan Patterns
_metadata.description: Regex-based static analysis patterns for OWASP Top 10:2021, OWASP API Security Top 10:2023, OWASP LLM Top 10:2025, CWE Top 25:2024, and Burp Suite detection categories. Targets Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, and C/C++. Pattern matching only — not a substitute for full SAST or professional security review.

misc.label: Additional Security Patterns
misc.description: Timing attacks, XXE, ReDoS, prototype pollution, format strings

patterns.name: CORS wildcard origin
patterns.description: CORS policy allows any origin (*). Permits cross-origin requests from attacker-controlled sites.
patterns.remediation: Restrict Access-Control-Allow-Origin to specific trusted domains; never use wildcard with credentials.

patterns.name: JWT decoded without verification
patterns.description: JWT decoded with signature verification disabled. Token claims are not authenticated.
patterns.remediation: Never pass verify=False to jwt.decode(). Always verify tokens before trusting claims.

patterns.name: JWT none algorithm acceptance
patterns.description: JWT library configured to accept 'none' algorithm — tokens with no signature will be accepted.
patterns.remediation: Explicitly allowlist only RS256 or ES256 in algorithms parameter. Never include 'none'.

patterns.name: Direct object reference from request param
patterns.description: Object ID read directly from request parameters without visible authorization check. Potential IDOR/BOLA.
patterns.remediation: After reading the ID, verify the requesting user is authorized to access that object before returning data.

patterns.name: MD5 used as cryptographic hash
patterns.description: MD5 is cryptographically broken. Collision attacks are practical.
patterns.remediation: Replace MD5 with SHA-256 or SHA-3 for integrity checks, or Argon2id/bcrypt for password hashing.

patterns.name: SHA-1 used as cryptographic hash
patterns.description: SHA-1 is deprecated for cryptographic use since 2017 SHAttered collision.
patterns.remediation: Use SHA-256, SHA-384, or SHA-512 instead.

patterns.name: Hardcoded encryption key or IV
patterns.description: Cryptographic key or IV hardcoded as a string literal.
patterns.remediation: Load keys from environment variables or a key management service (KMS).

patterns.name: Insecure random for security purpose
patterns.description: Non-cryptographic random function used. Output is predictable.
patterns.remediation: Use secrets.token_hex() in Python or crypto.randomBytes() in Node.js for security-sensitive randomness.

patterns.name: ECB cipher mode
patterns.description: ECB mode leaks patterns in ciphertext. Identical plaintext blocks produce identical ciphertext blocks.
patterns.remediation: Use AES-GCM or AES-CBC with random IV. Never use ECB.

patterns.name: SSL/TLS verification disabled
patterns.description: TLS certificate verification disabled. Connection is vulnerable to MITM attacks.
patterns.remediation: Never disable TLS verification in production. Use proper certificate chains.

patterns.name: SQL injection via string concatenation (Python)
patterns.description: SQL query built by string concatenation or %-formatting. Classic SQL injection vector.
patterns.remediation: Use parameterized queries: cursor.execute(sql, (param,)). Never concatenate user input into SQL.

patterns.name: SQL injection via template literal (JS/TS)
patterns.description: SQL query built with template literal interpolation. SQL injection risk.
patterns.remediation: Use parameterized queries or an ORM with bound parameters.

patterns.name: eval() with non-literal argument
patterns.description: eval() called with a non-literal argument. Remote code execution if argument contains user input.
patterns.remediation: Eliminate eval() entirely. Use JSON.parse() for data, proper parsers for expressions.

patterns.name: exec() with non-literal argument (Python)
patterns.description: Python exec() called with a dynamic argument. Executes arbitrary Python code.
patterns.remediation: Remove exec() from production code. Use ast.literal_eval for safe evaluation.

patterns.name: innerHTML / outerHTML assignment
patterns.description: innerHTML or outerHTML assignment can introduce XSS if the value contains user-controlled content.
patterns.remediation: Use textContent for plain text. Use DOMPurify.sanitize() before innerHTML if HTML is required.

patterns.name: dangerouslySetInnerHTML (React)
patterns.description: React dangerouslySetInnerHTML bypasses XSS protection.
patterns.remediation: Sanitize with DOMPurify before passing to dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

patterns.name: NoSQL injection (MongoDB operator in user input)
patterns.description: MongoDB query built directly from request object. Attacker can inject $gt, $ne operators.
patterns.remediation: Validate and allowlist query fields. Use schema validation (Zod) before passing to MongoDB.

patterns.name: Debug mode enabled in production config
patterns.description: Debug mode active. Exposes stack traces, internal paths, and potentially a remote debugger.
patterns.remediation: Set DEBUG=False. Never ship debug=True in production builds.

patterns.name: Unrestricted file upload without extension check
patterns.description: File saved to disk without visible extension or MIME type validation.
patterns.remediation: Validate file extension against an allowlist, verify MIME type server-side, store outside webroot.

patterns.name: Hardcoded password
patterns.description: Password hardcoded as string literal in source code.
patterns.remediation: Move to environment variable or secrets manager. Rotate the exposed credential immediately.

patterns.name: Hardcoded API key or token
patterns.description: API key or auth token hardcoded in source.
patterns.remediation: Load from environment variables. Add to .gitignore and rotate the key.

patterns.name: Stack trace exposed in HTTP response
patterns.description: Stack trace sent in HTTP response. Exposes internal file paths and code structure.
patterns.remediation: Log stack traces server-side only. Return generic error messages to clients.

patterns.name: Unsafe Python deserializer (pickle/dill/marshal)
patterns.description: Unsafe Python deserialization function. Deserializing attacker-controlled data executes arbitrary code.
patterns.remediation: Never deserialize data from untrusted sources with pickle/dill/marshal. Use json.loads().

patterns.name: PyYAML unsafe load
patterns.description: yaml.load() without SafeLoader enables remote code execution via crafted YAML.
patterns.remediation: Replace yaml.load(data) with yaml.safe_load(data).

patterns.name: Weak password hashing (MD5/SHA1 for passwords)
patterns.description: Password hashed with MD5 or SHA1. Reversible in seconds with GPU attacks.
patterns.remediation: Use bcrypt (12+ rounds), scrypt, or Argon2id for password hashing.

patterns.name: JWT hardcoded secret
patterns.description: JWT signed with hardcoded string secret. Attacker with source access can forge tokens.
patterns.remediation: Load JWT secret from environment variable. Use RS256 with a proper private key.

patterns.name: Session secret hardcoded
patterns.description: Session secret hardcoded in source. Attacker can forge session cookies.
patterns.remediation: Load SECRET_KEY from environment variable. Ensure it is at least 32 cryptographically random bytes.

patterns.name: LangChain dangerous deserialization flag
patterns.description: LangChain dangerous deserialization explicitly enabled. Loading untrusted chain files can execute arbitrary code.
patterns.remediation: Never set allow_dangerous_deserialization=True with untrusted input.

patterns.name: Exception silently swallowed
patterns.description: Bare except/pass silently swallows exceptions. Security errors become invisible.
patterns.remediation: Log all caught exceptions with context. Never use bare pass in except blocks.

patterns.name: Sensitive data in log statement
patterns.description: Sensitive field name appears in a log statement. Credentials or PII may be written to logs.
patterns.remediation: Redact sensitive fields before logging. Log events, not credential values.

patterns.name: SSRF via user-controlled URL (Python requests)
patterns.description: HTTP request made with a variable URL parameter. If URL comes from user input, this is SSRF.
patterns.remediation: Validate URLs against an allowlist. Block internal IPs (169.254.x.x, 10.x, 192.168.x, 127.x).

patterns.name: SSRF via user-controlled URL (JS fetch/axios)
patterns.description: HTTP request with dynamic URL variable. If URL is user-supplied, enables SSRF.
patterns.remediation: Allowlist permitted URLs/domains. Validate URL scheme and host before making requests.

patterns.name: API endpoint returns full object without field filtering
patterns.description: Entire database object returned in API response. May expose fields the caller should not see.
patterns.remediation: Explicitly select only the fields needed. Never return raw ORM objects to clients.

patterns.name: Mass assignment via spread/Object.assign from request body
patterns.description: Request body spread directly to an object. Attackers can set fields like isAdmin or role.
patterns.remediation: Use an explicit allowlist of accepted fields. Never spread the entire request body onto a model.

patterns.name: No rate limit on auth endpoint
patterns.description: Auth POST endpoint defined without visible rate limiting. Enables brute force.
patterns.remediation: Apply rate limiting (express-rate-limit) before auth endpoints. Limit to 5-10 requests per minute.

patterns.name: Admin endpoint without role check
patterns.description: Admin endpoint defined. Verify role check middleware is applied.
patterns.remediation: Apply role-based middleware (requireAdmin) before all admin route handlers.

patterns.name: Default CORS origin:* in middleware
patterns.description: CORS middleware configured with origin:*. API accessible from any website.
patterns.remediation: Set CORS origin to a specific domain allowlist.

patterns.name: Prompt built with f-string user input (Python)
patterns.description: LLM prompt constructed by f-string interpolation of user-controlled variable. Enables prompt injection.
patterns.remediation: Use structured message objects [{role, content}]. Sanitize user content in the user role only.

patterns.name: Prompt built with template literal user input (JS/TS)
patterns.description: LLM prompt built via template literal with user-controlled variable. Enables prompt injection.
patterns.remediation: Use structured chat messages [{role:'user', content: userInput}]. Never interpolate into system prompts.

patterns.name: System prompt string concatenation
patterns.description: System prompt modified by concatenating user-controlled content.
patterns.remediation: Keep system prompts static. Place user content exclusively in user-role messages.

patterns.name: LLM API key hardcoded in client constructor
patterns.description: LLM API key hardcoded in client constructor call.
patterns.remediation: Load API key from environment variable: api_key=os.environ['OPENAI_API_KEY'].

patterns.name: os.system/subprocess with LLM output
patterns.description: Shell command executed using LLM model output. Prompt injection leads to arbitrary code execution.
patterns.remediation: Never pass LLM output directly to shell commands. Parse to structured data, validate before acting.

patterns.name: No max_tokens set on LLM API call
patterns.description: LLM API call without max_tokens limit. Unbounded generation can cause runaway costs or DoS.
patterns.remediation: Always set max_tokens appropriate for the use case. Add per-user token budgets.

patterns.name: strcpy without bounds check (C)
patterns.description: strcpy() has no length limit. Buffer overflow with attacker-controlled data.
patterns.remediation: Replace strcpy() with strncpy(dst, src, sizeof(dst)-1) or strlcpy().

patterns.name: gets() use (C)
patterns.description: gets() reads unlimited input into a fixed buffer. Removed from C11. Always causes buffer overflow.
patterns.remediation: Replace gets() with fgets(buf, sizeof(buf), stdin).

patterns.name: sprintf without bounds (C)
patterns.description: sprintf() writes to buffer without length limit. Buffer overflow risk.
patterns.remediation: Replace sprintf() with snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), fmt, ...).

patterns.name: SQL string formatting with %s (Python)
patterns.description: SQL query uses %-formatting — this is NOT parameterized. SQL injection.
patterns.remediation: Use cursor.execute(sql, (params,)) with separate params tuple.

patterns.name: Path traversal via os.path.join with user input
patterns.description: os.path.join() with user-controlled component. Absolute path components override the base.
patterns.remediation: Call os.path.realpath() on the result and verify it starts with the intended base directory.

patterns.name: Array access with user-controlled index (C)
patterns.description: Array indexed by atoi/strtol on user input without bounds check. Out-of-bounds read.
patterns.remediation: Validate index is within [0, array_length) before use.

patterns.name: Use-after-free pattern (C)
patterns.description: Pointer used after free() call. Undefined behavior, exploitable.
patterns.remediation: Set pointer to NULL immediately after free(). Use smart pointers in C++.

patterns.name: File upload without MIME/extension validation
patterns.description: File upload handler detected. Without validation, attackers upload executable shells.
patterns.remediation: Validate file extension and MIME type against allowlist. Store outside webroot. Rename uploaded files.

patterns.name: Server-side template injection risk
patterns.description: Template engine called with user-controlled content. SSTI enables full code execution.
patterns.remediation: Never pass user input to template constructors. Use render_template() with static files only.

patterns.name: Deserialization from HTTP request body
patterns.description: Python deserialization called on HTTP request body. Arbitrary code execution.
patterns.remediation: Never deserialize pickle/marshal from untrusted HTTP input. Use JSON or protobuf.

patterns.name: NULL pointer dereference after malloc (C)
patterns.description: Memory allocated then immediately dereferenced without NULL check.
patterns.remediation: Always check malloc() return value for NULL before dereferencing.

patterns.name: Integer overflow in buffer allocation (C)
patterns.description: malloc/calloc with arithmetic expression. Integer overflow causes undersized buffer.
patterns.remediation: Use checked arithmetic. Prefer calloc(n, size) over malloc(n*size).

patterns.name: Authentication bypass via type coercion
patterns.description: Authentication comparison using == (loose equality). Type coercion can allow bypass.
patterns.remediation: Use === for all authentication comparisons. Use crypto.timingSafeEqual() for tokens.

patterns.name: State-changing GET endpoint
patterns.description: GET endpoint performs state-changing operations. GETs should be idempotent and are CSRF-vulnerable.
patterns.remediation: Move state-changing operations to POST/PUT/DELETE with CSRF tokens.

patterns.name: OS command injection via subprocess shell=True
patterns.description: subprocess called with shell=True. OS command injection if any argument contains user input.
patterns.remediation: Remove shell=True. Pass arguments as a list: subprocess.run(['cmd', arg1, arg2]).

patterns.name: os.system with variable argument
patterns.description: os.system() called with a variable argument. OS command injection.
patterns.remediation: Replace os.system() with subprocess.run(args_list).

patterns.name: Path traversal via unsanitized file path
patterns.description: File open/read with user-controlled path. Enables directory traversal.
patterns.remediation: Resolve with os.path.realpath() then verify it starts with the allowed base directory.

patterns.name: HTTP (non-TLS) URL in outbound call
patterns.description: Outbound HTTP call (non-TLS) to external host. Data transmitted in cleartext.
patterns.remediation: Use HTTPS for all outbound connections.

patterns.name: Weak TLS/SSL version specified
patterns.description: Deprecated SSL/TLS version specified. Vulnerable to POODLE, BEAST.
patterns.remediation: Use TLS 1.2 minimum, TLS 1.3 preferred.

patterns.name: Cookie without Secure or HttpOnly flag
patterns.description: Cookie set without Secure and/or HttpOnly flags.
patterns.remediation: Set httponly=True, secure=True, samesite='Strict' on all session cookies.

patterns.name: Open redirect via user-controlled URL
patterns.description: HTTP redirect destination derived from request parameter. Enables phishing via open redirect.
patterns.remediation: Validate redirect URL against allowlist of safe paths/domains.

patterns.name: TODO/FIXME with security context in source
patterns.description: Source comment documents a security shortcut or bypass.
patterns.remediation: Track security TODOs in the issue tracker, not source comments.

patterns.name: Timing attack risk in token comparison
patterns.description: Token compared with == or ===. String comparison is not constant-time.
patterns.remediation: Use hmac.compare_digest() in Python or crypto.timingSafeEqual() in Node.js.

patterns.name: XML external entity (XXE) risk
patterns.description: XML parsing with default settings. Most XML parsers resolve external entities by default.
patterns.remediation: Use defusedxml in Python, disable expandEntities in JS parsers.

patterns.name: Regular expression from user input (ReDoS risk)
patterns.description: Regex compiled from user input. Attacker can supply catastrophically backtracking pattern.
patterns.remediation: Never compile regex from user input. Use a safe regex engine or timeout.

patterns.name: Prototype pollution risk
patterns.description: Direct write to __proto__ or Object.prototype. Prototype pollution affects all objects.
patterns.remediation: Use Object.create(null) for dictionaries. Sanitize JSON keys to block __proto__.

patterns.name: Format string vulnerability (C)
patterns.description: printf-family function with user-controlled format string. Memory read/write via %n, %x.
patterns.remediation: Always use a literal format string: printf("%s", user_input).

patterns.name: Shell metacharacters via popen (C)
patterns.description: C popen() with user-controlled string. Shell metacharacters execute arbitrary commands.
patterns.remediation: Avoid popen() with user input. Use execv() family with argument arrays.

patterns.name: Insecure temp file creation
patterns.description: tmpnam/tempnam/mktemp create TOCTOU race condition. Symlink attacks possible.
patterns.remediation: Use mkstemp() in C or tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile() in Python.

patterns.name: Private key in source file
patterns.description: PEM private key literal in source file.
patterns.remediation: Remove key from source immediately. Store in a secrets manager. Rotate the key.
